model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that visits every city exactly once and returns to the start. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from the first to the second, and travelling a leg incurs a known cost that depends on the pair. The goal is to choose the legs so that the total travel cost of the trip is as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities that must all be visited on a single closed tour

sets.name: N
sets.doc: an index over every group of cities that forms a nonempty strict subset of all the cities, that is every group that contains at least one city but not all of them; each member of this set names one such group, and the group of cities it stands for can be recovered by enumerating all such subsets in a fixed order

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city, for each ordered pair of cities; the cost from a city to itself is zero and is never used

vars.name: x
vars.doc: one if the tour goes directly from the first city to the second city, zero otherwise; the entry from a city to itself is fixed to zero so no city links to itself

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total travel cost of the chosen tour

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every city is left exactly once on the trip. For each city, exactly one direct leg departs from it to some other city.

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Every city is entered exactly once on the trip. For each city, exactly one direct leg arrives at it from some other city.

constraints.name: z
constraints.intent: The total travel cost accumulates the cost of every leg that is actually used. Set the total cost equal to the sum, over all ordered pairs of cities, of the cost of going from the first city to the second multiplied by whether that leg is travelled.

constraints.name: I)
constraints.intent: The trip must form one connected round trip rather than splitting into several separate smaller loops. To rule this out, take any group of cities that is not empty and does not include all the cities. At least one used leg must go from a city inside that group to a city outside it, so the trip always crosses out of every such group. This must hold for every possible group of this kind.

query: Roadworks are about to close the link between city 4 and city 5 in both directions for the season. With that connection unavailable, what does the best route cost?

query: We're looking at a scenario where the depot at city 6 loses its direct connections to both city 1 and city 2 — no leg may run between 6 and either of them in either direction. What does the best tour come to then?

query: Suppose the direct road between city 2 and city 3 is taken out of service both ways. Drop that connection and tell me how the route distance shifts.

query: The route threads straight through the whole 1-2-3 cluster, chaining all three neighbours back to back. Wouldn't it be healthier to break that up and use at most one leg inside the 1-2-3 group? What's the model seeing that makes it want to chain them?
expected_pyomo: model.spread_123 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x[a, b] for a in ['i1', 'i2', 'i3'] for b in ['i1', 'i2', 'i3']) <= 1)

query: The plan keeps leaning on the direct shortcut between city 1 and city 3. That feels like a single point of failure — couldn't we serve the loop without ever using a direct 1-3 leg? What's the trade-off keeping it on that shortcut?
